---
title: "CCHS Analytic Dataset: Univariate Distributions"
subtitle: "Data Primer 1 \u2014 Complete Variable Reference by Section"
author: "Analysis Team"
date: today
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: yeti
    highlight-style: breeze
    code-line-numbers: false
    self-contained: true
    embed-resources: true
    keep-md: false
editor: visual
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Mission

This document provides a complete univariate profile of every variable in the `cchs_analytic` analysis-ready table produced by `manipulation/2-ellis.R`. It serves as the **canonical data reference** for all EDAs and Reports in this project.

**Dataset:** CCHS 2010 + 2014 pooled analytic sample\
**Rows:** 63,843 respondents **Cols:** 69 variables\
**Source:** `data-private/derived/cchs-2-tables/cchs_analytic.parquet`\
**Pipeline documentation:** `data-public/metadata/CACHE-manifest.md`\
**Variable traceability:** [Variable Inclusion](variable-inclusion.html)

All computation lives in `univariate-distributions.R` (the laboratory). This document sources those chunks and presents output only. All chunk bodies are intentionally empty.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
#| label: knitr-setup
#| code-summary: Reconciles working directory between execution modes
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
#| warning: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
library(knitr)
opts_knit$set(root.dir = "../../") # keep isolated from surrounding chunks
```

```{r}
#| label: set-options
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
#| message: false
cat("Working directory: ", getwd())
report_render_start_time <- Sys.time()
opts_chunk$set(
  results  = "show",
  message  = FALSE,
  warning  = FALSE,
  comment  = NA,
  tidy     = FALSE
)
options(width = 110)
# display-* chunks: hide code and pass output as raw HTML/markdown to pandoc
knitr::opts_hooks$set(label = function(options) {
  if (isTRUE(grepl("^display-", options$label))) {
    options$echo    <- FALSE
    options$results <- "asis"
  }
  options
})
read_chunk("analysis/data-primer-1/univariate-distributions.R")
```

# Environment

```{r load-packages}
```

```{r load-sources}
```

```{r declare-globals}
```

```{r declare-functions}
```

# Data

```{r load-data}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Data Context

```{r}
#| label: data-context-overview
#| echo: false
#| message: false
# Overview table: rows, cols, completeness flags
d_local <- readRDS("analysis/data-primer-1/data-local/univariate-distributions.rds")
cat(sprintf(
  "Parquet table: cchs_analytic\nRows: 63,843  |  Columns: 69\nCycles: CCHS 2010 + CCHS 2014 (pooled)\nSource: data-private/derived/cchs-2-tables/cchs_analytic.parquet\n"
))
knitr::kable(
  d_local$t_cycle_freq,
  col.names = c("CCHS cycle", "N", "% (of valid)"),
  caption   = "Sample size by survey cycle"
)
```

```{r}
#| label: data-context-completeness
#| echo: false
#| message: false
if (nrow(d_local$t_flags_summary) > 0) {
  knitr::kable(
    d_local$t_flags_summary,
    col.names = c("Completeness flag", "N TRUE", "N FALSE", "N missing"),
    caption   = "Analytic completeness flags"
  )
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 1. Outcomes

## 1.1 Primary and Sensitivity Outcomes

`days_absent_total` is the primary outcome: total workdays absent due to any health reason in the past 2 weeks (LOP module). `days_absent_chronic` counts only absences attributable to chronic conditions.

```{r section-outcomes}
```

```{r display-outcomes}
```

## 1.2 LOP Component Variables

The eight raw LOP components contributing to `days_absent_total`. Each measures days absent for a specific health reason. High missingness is structural: the LOP module is administered only to currently employed respondents who reported any absence.

```{r display-lop}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 2. Chronic Conditions (Primary Exposure)

Seventeen of the 19 requested chronic conditions are available in the PUMF. Two are suppressed for confidentiality (`ccc_300` — other mental illness; `ccc_185` — digestive disease) and are absent from this dataset. All `cc_*` columns are **logical** (TRUE/FALSE); prevalence percentages exclude missing values.

```{r section-chronic-conditions}
```

```{r display-chronic-conditions}
```

> **Note:** Two conditions from §2.2 are absent from the PUMF and not shown: *other mental illness* (`ccc_300`) and *digestive disease* (`ccc_185`). Both require RDC access.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 3. Predisposing Variables

Predisposing variables characterise the respondent's socio-demographic background. Student status (`sdcdgstud`) is suppressed in the PUMF and renders as all-NA. `dhhdfc12p` (children ≥ 12 yrs) has no PUMF equivalent and is absent. See [Variable Inclusion](variable-inclusion.html) §4 for full traceability.

```{r section-predisposing}
```

::: panel-tabset
### Age Group

```{r display-age-group}
```

### Sex

```{r display-sex}
```

### Marital Status

```{r display-marital-status}
```

### Household Size

```{r display-household-size}
```

### Education

```{r display-education}
```

### Immigration Status

```{r display-immigration-status}
```

### Visible Minority

```{r display-visible-minority}
```

### Living Arrangements

```{r display-living-arrangements}
```

### Student Status

```{r display-student-status}
```

### Raw Age Code

```{r display-dhhgage}
```

### Children in Household

```{r display-children}
```
:::

> **Note on missing household variables:** `dhhdfc12p` (children ≥ 12 yrs) has no PUMF equivalent. See [Variable Inclusion](variable-inclusion.html) §4.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 4. Facilitating Variables

Facilitating variables describe health-system access, health behaviours, and labour-market context. All 12 facilitating variables are populated in the current pipeline output. See [Variable Inclusion](variable-inclusion.html) §5 for traceability.

```{r section-facilitating}
```

::: panel-tabset
### Income

```{r display-income}
```

### Family Doctor

```{r display-family-doctor}
```

### Employment Type

```{r display-employment-type}
```

### Work Schedule

```{r display-work-schedule}
```

### Smoking Status

```{r display-smoking}
```

### BMI Category

```{r display-bmi-category}
```

### Physical Activity

```{r display-physical-activity}
```

### Alcohol Use

```{r display-alcohol}
```

### Fruit & Veg

```{r display-fruit-veg}
```

### Occupation Category

```{r display-occupation-category}
```

### Work Stress

```{r display-work-stress}
```

### Province

```{r display-province}
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 5. Needs Variables

Needs variables capture perceived health status and functional limitations. ADL items use semantic names after Ellis renaming (`adl_01` → `adl_meals`, etc.). All ADL items are binary Yes/No factors.

```{r section-needs}
```

::: panel-tabset
### General Health

```{r display-health-perceived}
```

### Mental Health

```{r display-mental-health}
```

### Health vs. Prior Year

```{r display-health-vs-prior-year}
```

### Injured Past 12 Months

```{r display-injury}
```

### ADL Items

```{r display-adl}
```
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 6. Survey Design Variables

```{r section-survey-design}
```

## 6.1 Sampling Weights

`wts_m` is the per-cycle survey weight; `wts_m_pooled` is the pooled weight adjusted for two-cycle pooling (divide by 2 per Statistics Canada guidelines).

```{r display-weights}
```

## 6.2 Survey Cycle

```{r display-cycle}
```

## 6.3 Completeness Flags

Three logical flags indicate whether a respondent has complete data for each analytical dimension. Use `flag_analytic_complete` to subset to the analytic sample.

```{r display-flags}
```

**`flag_complete_ccc`** — All 17 chronic condition indicators (`cc_*`) are non-`NA`. `cc_copd` was administered to a sub-sample only (\~66% coverage), so this flag is `TRUE` for 42,064 of the 63,843 analytic rows. Use this flag when running models that include any `cc_*` predictor.

**`flag_complete_predictors`** — All key predictor variables (age, sex, education, income, immigration status, province, employment, smoking, BMI, physical activity, perceived health, etc.) are non-`NA`. Rows with missing values on any single predictor will have this flag set to `FALSE`. Covers 59,309 rows.

**`flag_analytic_complete`** — Both `flag_complete_ccc` AND `flag_complete_predictors` are `TRUE`. This is the recommended subsetting criterion for fully complete-case analyses. Covers 39,702 rows (\~62% of the analytic sample).

> All three flags are pre-computed in the Ellis script. No need to re-derive exclusion logic in downstream analyses — simply filter on the relevant flag.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 7. Clustered Missingness Audit

> **Context (Marc-André, 2026-05-15)**: Several variables share a similar level of
> missingness (~30%). This section audits whether these form a coherent cluster and
> documents the mechanism for each — primarily age-gated questions that were only
> administered to respondents above a certain age threshold.

## 7.1 Age-Gated Variables

The following variables were administered conditionally based on respondent age:

| Variable | Threshold | Mechanism | Coverage |
|----------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| `cc_copd` (CCC_091) | 35+ years (DHHGAGE ≥ 8) | COPD question not asked to younger respondents | ~66% |
| `bmi_category` (HWTGISW) | 18+ years (DHHGAGE ≥ 3) | BMI derived only for adults 18+ | ~95% |

## 7.2 Other Missingness Mechanisms

| Variable | Mechanism | Notes |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| `income_hh` (INCGHH) | Non-response / refusal | Income is sensitive; ~5% refuse |
| `immigration_status` | NOT STATED in source | ~3% codes 9/99 → NA |
| `occupation_category` (LBSGSOC) | Not applicable for some employment types | Self-employed with no SOC code |
| `fruit_veg_daily` (FVCGTOT) | Partial module administration | ~10% missing across cycles |
| `smoking_status` (SMKDSTY) | Complex derived variable | Requires valid responses to multiple source items |

## 7.3 Interpretation

The ~30% missingness cluster is dominated by `cc_copd` (age-gated, 34% missing). When
this condition is excluded from the complete-case flag, `flag_complete_ccc` would rise from
66% to approximately 95%. Analysts working exclusively with conditions other than COPD
should consider subsetting on the 16 remaining conditions rather than using
`flag_complete_ccc` which penalizes younger respondents.

For COPD-inclusive models, restrict to respondents aged 35+ (DHHGAGE ≥ 8) and impute
`cc_copd = FALSE` for the younger subsample, following the recommendation from the
2026-05-15 review session.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{r}
#| label: session-info
#| echo: false
#| results: hide
report_render_stop_time <- Sys.time()
cat(
  "Report rendered at:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),
  "\nElapsed:", round(difftime(report_render_stop_time, report_render_start_time, units = "secs"), 1),
  "seconds\n"
)
sessionInfo()
```
